title: Large Language Models as Tool Makers
summary: The paper proposes LATM (LLMs As Tool Makers), a framework where LLMs create their own tools (as Python functions) to solve tasks, and then a lighter LLM uses these tools. This two-phase approach improves performance and reduces inference cost.

key_claims.claim: LATM enables LLMs to create reusable Python tools that improve task-solving accuracy on reasoning benchmarks.

evidence.description: Accuracy improvements on BigBench tasks compared to CoT.

methods.name: LATM Framework
methods.description: Two-phase workflow: Tool Making (generation of Python scripts) and Tool Using (execution by a dispatcher).

limitations: Requires executing Python code generated by the LLM, introducing security risks.
Tool creation phase uses a large, expensive model (e.g., GPT-4).
applicability_to_tars: High. The concept of LLMs generating tools perfectly aligns with Tars.Evolution where the loop promotes implementation snippets into helper tools.
applicability_to_ix: Medium. IX can serve as the lightweight executor/dispatcher for the tools created by the TARS brain.
applicability_to_demerzel: Low. Does not directly impact governance.

suggested_followups.title: Investigate Python execution sandbox for TARS tool making
suggested_followups.description: Evaluate incorporating an isolated sandbox where TARS can generate and test temporary Python or F# tools before promoting them via the evolution pipeline.

provenance: Ingested via manual agent review by Jules on 2026-06-28.
